questions.question: Design a program to implement the sliding window protocol in the data link layer for a simple network scenario where two devices (Device A and Device B) are connected through a wired network. The program should handle the sending and receiving of packets using the sliding window protocol. Assume a packet size of 1000 bits and a maximum window size of 5 packets. Device A should send packets to Device B, and Device B should acknowledge each packet received.

Write a program in Python to simulate a sliding window protocol in the data link layer. The program should allow two devices (Device A and Device B) to communicate by sending and receiving packets. Implement the following features: packet size, maximum window size, and packet acknowledgement. The program should print out the sequence number of each packet sent and received, as well as the acknowledgement sequence number for each packet received.

Suppose you are designing a network protocol to implement the sliding window protocol for a network with a large number of devices. To handle congestion and packet loss, implement a feedback mechanism that adjusts the maximum window size based on the average round-trip time (RTT) of the devices. If the RTT increases, reduce the maximum window size by 20%. If the RTT decreases, increase the maximum window size by 20%. Implement this mechanism in a program and evaluate its effectiveness in reducing congestion and packet loss.
